values_hierarchy.md orders: (1) constitutional integrity/protocol safety, (2) treasury stewardship/downside protection, (3) evidence quality, (4) public-benefit ecosystem growth.

Content is stated in the doctrine: 'judge directionally on the available public record with a caution penalty (dossier soft gate...) — in practice NO or ABSTAIN on thin asks.' Minor citation imprecision only: this language lives in values_hierarchy.md's conflict-handling defaults (which itself references treasury_spending_doctrine.json v1.1.0+), not in the Dossier Gate Posture section of treasury_spending_doctrine.md. A mislabeled section pointer with accurate content is not a material discrepancy.
Prioritization section: 'proposals are not funded by submission order. They are funded by DRep approval weight, descending' within the available budget.

verification.summary: All 27 facts are supported by the source material with exact matches on every checked number (540,750 ADA / 540750000000 lovelace; 420,000 + 105,000; 525,000 + 15,750; 654/288/79/58; 30/26/4; 67%; 350M NCL over epochs 613–713; 14 proposals / 57273260000000 lovelace; yes=50/no=11/abstain=3; inflow 158838297854652 / withdrawals 361435336000000 / ratio 2.275492; epochs 638/645), every quoted string, and all three smart-contract addresses verified character-for-character. No contradicted or not_found facts and no invented specifics. One non-material note: fact 26 attributes the soft-gate NO/ABSTAIN language to treasury_spending_doctrine.md's Dossier Gate Posture section, but the quoted guidance actually appears in values_hierarchy.md's conflict-handling defaults; the content itself is accurate, so no material discrepancy is recorded.
